g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about securing database services in cloud computing - essential knowledge for modern IT professionals.
g.text: Security of Database Services in Cloud Computing

g.data-narration: As organizations migrate sensitive data to the cloud, securing databases becomes critical. A single breach can cause catastrophic data loss and financial damage.
The cloud provider secures the infrastructure while customers protect their data and access configurations. Understanding this division is fundamental.
Virtual Private Clouds isolate your databases in private subnets with no direct internet route, forming the first line of defense.
Security Groups act as virtual firewalls at the instance level while NACLs provide subnet-level protection, creating layered network defense.
Encryption at rest protects stored data using TDE with KMS keys, while encryption in transit uses SSL/TLS to secure data moving between systems.
g.text: Data Encryption: At Rest & In Transit

g.data-narration: IAM enforces least privilege with fine-grained permissions. Integrate database access with cloud IAM and enable MFA for enhanced security.
Database auditing tracks all activities through CloudTrail and native logs, essential for compliance, forensics, and detecting suspicious behavior.
g.text: Log entries: LOGIN | SELECT | INSERT | UPDATE | DELETE

g.data-narration: Four major threats target cloud databases: misconfiguration (open ports), data breaches, DoS attacks, and SQL injection. Each has specific mitigations.
Remember: Shared responsibility means provider secures infrastructure while you secure data. Use defense-in-depth with encryption, IAM, and monitoring.
g.text: At rest + In transit with KMS
